Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska and Matt Newborg work together to share some of their best sanding techniques that are guaranteed to save you time in the shop. Find out which sanding techniques work best with different types of wood, learn the difference between various types of sandpaper, and see which tools are available for your sanding needs.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Choosing the Right Sanding Method

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Author Laura Poplin combines the ancient art of leatherwork and chainmaille to make beautiful contemporary jewelry. Learn to make colorful dyed and painted leather rectangles and finish them into jewelry using classic leatherworking techniques. Laura shows how to connect leather pieces with Byzantine chainmaile weave to make a cuff style bracelet. Katie demonstrates a quick and easy cup chain bracelet.
Video.title: Leather and Chainmaille Jewelry

Video.summary: Bring the fun to any gathering and impress your guests with handmade wooden games you can make right in the shop. George Vondriska shows you how to make a game board for chess and checkers, as well as a brain-teasing ring stacking game to challenge your friends.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Chess/Checker Board & Ring Stacking Games

Video.summary: Look across the Pacific Ocean for inspiration. Artist and world traveler Luana Rubin shares quilts from the Tokyo Quilt Festival and discusses global fabric and color trends. Free motion quilt artist Christina Cameli uses a walking foot and free motion stitches to make grid-based designs. Graphic artist and quilter Sheri Cifaldi-Morill appliquĂ©s traditional Hawaiian motifs with a modern twist.
Video.title: Pacific Inspiration

Video.summary: Angela ponders on her new year's resolutions as she builds a curved log cabin quilt using ombré fabric.
Video.title: Curved Log Cabin Quilt

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: Angela's got stars in her eyes this week as she pieces together the eight-pointed star Twinkling Twilight quilt by Nancy Smith.
Video.title: Twinkling Twilight

Video.summary: How do you tackle a quilt with 512 half-square triangles? With the quickest HST-cutting technique this side of the Mississippi, courtesy of the Midnight Quilter.
Video.title: King-Size Color Crystals Quilt

Video.summary: Angela's back and bursting with love. In this season premiere, she uses half-square triangles to make a modern bursting star quilt while sharing her tips on how to keep a lasting relationship with a quilter.
Video.title: Modern Bursting Star Quilt

Video.summary: From cornstarch to cuticle pushers, Angela shares five ingenious hacks for using common household items for quilting.
Video.title: Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting

Image.alt: Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting
Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting
Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting

Video.summary: How do you tackle a quilt with 512 half-square triangles? With the quickest HST-cutting technique this side of the Mississippi, courtesy of the Midnight Quilter.
Video.title: King-Size Color Crystals Quilt

Video.summary: On the season finale of Season 6 of the Midnight Quilt Show, follow along as Angela shares her clever take on a braided quilt pattern she created herself!
Video.title: Braided Star Quilt

Video.summary: This one's a total strip show! (Well, sort of.) Angela's strip-piecing secrets can help you breeze through the blocks, sashing and border of a beautiful quilt.
Video.title: Four-Color Fruit Slices Quilt

Video.summary: In the final episode of the Holiday Gift-Along, your last-minute gift quilter, Angela Walters, is making a beginner-friendly criss cross quilt. This gift-able quilt project is much easier than it looks, so you still have time to make it before the holidays!
Video.title: Stash-Friendly Criss Cross Quilt

Video.summary: This week Angela is preparing to host a dinner for quilters, so of course, she has to quilt up a few placemats!
Video.title: Quilt-As-You-Go Placemats

Video.summary: Appliqué is the star: literally! Emily Thompson has the secret to fashionable kids' T-shirts with their favorite design. Then Rebecca Kemp Brent has a tutorial on appliqué using your machine. We finish with embroidered jewelry with Joanne Banko. Create a free standing lace design for instant fashion!
Video.title: Applique - Stars and More

Video.summary: All you need is a needle and thread to stitch your way to beautiful detailing with an embellished leather cuff bracelet using blanket stitch, bead embroidery on a block print with Jennifer Van Benschoten, and flat and circular square stitch with Jean Campbell.
Video.title: Stitch It

Video.summary: Look to nature for design inspiration. Using quartz points, designer Molly Schaller creates stunning necklaces and other jewelry. To work out, she makes jewelry from pearls in all shapes, colors and sizes. Artist Jill MacKay cools down with designs in die-cut leather. Katie Hacker discusses how bead-making can benefit children facing health challenges.
Video.title: Work Out Naturally

Video.summary: Set off in new directions. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer creates a travel journal with painted images. Cheryl Boglioli makes a handy caddy for creating on the road. Joe Rotella has a tip for keeping track of unmounted rubber stamps. Ashley Horton saves the most delicious travel memories on stenciled pages.
Video.title: Episode 8: Travel

Video.summary: Make your own special occasion with a unique card. First, Jenn Mason creates a photo card. Next, May Flaum uses a stamped image for her card design. Host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer interviews Kazan Clark. Then, Julie shows how to create a card background using photo corners. Last, Julie creates a card with a house shaped cut out.
Video.title: Episode 1: Cards

Video.summary: Get out of your artistic rut and look for new ideas to inspire you. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer shows a new style of pop up card. Then, Lynn Lilly has three fresh ways to personalize a gift. Finally, Tori Weyers has lesson two and experimenting with mediums. Alcohol based inks and acrylic, water-based inks -- together can have some uncontrollable but beautiful effects.
Video.title: Episode 3: Fresh Ideas

Video.summary: Timing is everything! Doing steps in the right order, waiting for a resin to set, letting glue dry -- these are issues we are all aware of. In this episode we look at projects where timing makes the difference. First is a foiling project, where order makes the difference. Then Dina Wakley has a paint technique using rubbing alcohol. Finally, Joe Rotella makes a wine cork shadow box personalized with a favorite sentiment.
Video.title: It's All in the Timing

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Hold onto your needles! Tracie and Jodi, are here to dish out fresh fiber inspiration. From project picks to wooly good gossip, things are about to get super knitty… and maybe just a little nutty.
Video.title: Episode 13

Video.summary: No cable needle? No problem. In this episode Tracie and Jodi demonstrate just how simple the Scania Shawl's mock cable really is. Just slip, knit, knit!
Video.title: SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables

Image.alt: SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables
SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables
SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables

Video.summary: No cable needle? No problem. In this episode Tracie and Jodi demonstrate just how simple the Scania Shawl's mock cable really is. Just slip, knit, knit!
Video.title: SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables

Image.alt: SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables
SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables
SCANIA KNIT ALONG II: How to Knit Mock Cables

Video.summary: So you’ve finished knitting your Novel-T, now what? Tune in to see how to weave in the ends when working with cotton yarn. Hint: this method involves fabric glue.
Video.title: T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends

Image.alt: T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends
T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends
T-Shirt Finishing Tips: Hems, Necklines + Weaving in Ends

Video.summary: Use what you make and get double fun from your crafts. Weave hip survival bracelets with some tips from Katie. Franz uses everyday objects to make a robot storage container. Blogger Rachel Howard and her kids make a miniature construction site for toy earth-moving equipment! Use items from the Family Craft Box and recycle old road maps to craft a travel case full of learning games and activities -- and treats, too!
Video.title: Use It

Video.summary: Today's word of the day is distortion. This is when we take one aspect of something and change it or make it different -- usually not the normal way we might look at something. Our first examples are abstract portraits using magazines. Then, create a fun house mirror using tiles. Our art journal technique uses plastic wrap with water colors to create unusual distorted backgrounds. Last, use reverse shape cutouts to make unique art works.
Video.title: Distortion

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: A New Look at Longarm Quilting
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
